clean_code:
from zipline.api import order_target_percent

def rebalance(context, data):
    n_long = len(context.long_assets)
    n_short = len(context.short_assets)

    # Target 100% gross exposure: 50% long, 50% short
    long_weight = 0.50 / n_long if n_long > 0 else 0
    short_weight = -0.50 / n_short if n_short > 0 else 0

    for asset in context.long_assets:
        order_target_percent(asset, long_weight)

    for asset in context.short_assets:
        order_target_percent(asset, short_weight)
description: Fixed weights applied to variable number of assets, creating unintended leverage.
explanation: Applying 50% to each of 3 long assets = 150% gross long exposure. Plus 30% to each of 2 short assets = 60% short. Total gross exposure = 210%. This is 2.1x leverage, which may violate margin requirements or risk limits. The error compounds as the number of assets changes dynamically.
suggested_fix: Always divide target weights by the number of assets to maintain intended gross exposure.

issues.title: Fixed weights applied to multiple assets creates unintended leverage
